content: Activity feeds in SaaS use SSE (server-sent events) as default for server-to-client notifications; WebSockets only when client also pushes frequently. Polling is anti-pattern. Fan-out via Redis pub/sub when multi-instance. Read/unread state tracked per-user in DB with a 'read_at' timestamp or separate junction table. Notification center UI: bell icon with unread badge count, dropdown/panel listing items newest-first, mark-all-read, dismiss individual. Infinite scroll or pagination for long feeds. Batch delivery prevents UI flicker. Client-side state management (catch missed events on reconnect) is harder than server side.
